name: Yamunotri
description: Yamunotri is the source of the Yamuna River and one of the four sacred Char Dham pilgrimage sites.

detailedInfo.introduction: Yamunotri is the source of the Yamuna River and one of the four sacred Char Dham pilgrimage sites.
detailedInfo.history: The temple is dedicated to Goddess Yamuna and is part of the Char Dham Yatra. It was built by Maharani Gularia of Jaipur in the 19th century.
detailedInfo.cultural_significance: It holds immense importance in Hindu pilgrimage traditions, representing the sacred purity of the Yamuna river.
detailedInfo.architecture: A simple yet beautiful temple structure surrounded by towering Himalayan peaks and glaciers.
detailedInfo.interestingFacts: Hot water springs like Surya Kund are used by pilgrims to cook rice and potatoes as prasad to take home.

culturalVault.history: Believed to be the home of Goddess Yamuna, daughter of the Sun God Surya.
culturalVault.stories: Bathing in the river here is said to cleanse one of their sins and protect against an untimely death.
culturalVault.myths: The hot water springs are said to be the blessings of the Sun God.

best_time_to_visit: May to June, September to October

name: Gangotri
description: Gangotri is the origin of the sacred River Ganga and an important pilgrimage site.

detailedInfo.introduction: Gangotri is the origin of the sacred River Ganga and an important pilgrimage site within the Char Dham circuit.
detailedInfo.history: Built in the 18th century by Amar Singh Thapa, a Gorkha commander.
detailedInfo.cultural_significance: The Ganga river is considered the most sacred river in Hinduism. Legend states Lord Shiva released the river from the locks of his hair here.
detailedInfo.architecture: The temple is a pristine white granite structure situated near the banks of the roaring Bhagirathi river.
detailedInfo.interestingFacts: The actual physical source of the river is the Gaumukh glacier, which requires a steep 19km trek from the Gangotri temple.

culturalVault.history: Discovered and established as a shrine by Amar Singh Thapa. Rebuilt later by the Jaipur dynasty.
culturalVault.stories: King Bhagirath mediated here to bring Goddess Ganga down to earth to wash away the sins of his ancestors.
culturalVault.myths: The sacred stone, Bhagirath Shila, is believed to be the exact spot where he mediated.

best_time_to_visit: May to June, September to October
